deep_dive.title: Cold, dark, and hungry: Dynamic transcriptional regulation across eight months of brumation
deep_dive.abstract: Long-term winter dormancy in ectotherms (brumation) defines the annual cycle of many temperate-zone reptiles, yet the transcriptional regulation that supports survival across months of cold and aphagy remains poorly understood. We generated time-resolved transcriptomic profiles of liver and testis from male red-sided garter snakes (Thamnophis sirtalis parietalis) at five timepoints spanning the eight-month brumation cycle: pre-brumation, early, mid-, and late brumation, and post-arousal under continued aphagy. Time-course negative-binomial regression (maSigPro) followed by gene-set enrichment analysis identified 3,715 transcripts in liver and 5,828 in testis with significant temporal expression structure organized into five overarching temporal patterns: sustained downregulation, downregulation with post-arousal recovery, sustained upregulation, brumation-specific upregulation and cyclic modulation. Liver showed coordinated upregulation of fatty acid mobilization enzymes (ATGL, FOXO1, PPAR, CPT1A) and gluconeogenic regulators (CREBBP, PCK1) coincident with sustained low temperatures. Additionally, low temperature transcriptional activity was suggestive of a shift toward hepatic lipid mobilization and alanine-supported gluconeogenesis. Testis showed sustained suppression of meiosis, reproduction, and DNA-metabolism gene sets that did not fully recover at arousal consistent with this species' dissociated reproductive pattern. Both tissues showed coordinated upregulation of stress-response pathways involving heat-shock proteins, HIF1 and a glutathione-based antioxidant defense. Interestingly, three vitellogenin transcripts and 17{beta}-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenases associated with estradiol-favoring steroid metabolism were upregulated in male liver during late brumation, which is not expected during natural physiology in adult males. Together these data support a framework in which temperature- and starvation-associated transcriptional programs contribute to survival of one of the longest, coldest brumations documented in a squamate.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: brumation is a hibernation-like state in ectotherms; paper studies transcriptional regulation during prolonged cold and aphagy
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究爬行动物冬眠（brumation）期间在寒冷和饥饿条件下的转录调控
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究爬行动物冬眠（brumation）期间在寒冷和饥饿条件下的转录调控
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究爬行动物冬眠（brumation）期间在寒冷和饥饿条件下的转录调控
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This study characterizes time-resolved liver and testis transcriptomes across eight months of brumation in garter snakes, revealing dynamic transcriptional programs that support survival under cold and food scarcity.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究对红边袜带蛇在八个月冬眠（brumation）期间的肝脏和睾丸进行了时间分辨转录组分析，揭示了在寒冷和食物短缺条件下维持生存的动态转录调控程序。作者在五个时间点采集样本，使用maSigPro时间序列回归方法鉴定出数千个差异表达基因，并将其组织成五个主要表达趋势。这些结果阐明了爬行动物长期冬眠的分子机制，为理解极端环境适应提供了新见解。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究对红边袜带蛇在八个月冬眠（brumation）期间的肝脏和睾丸进行了时间分辨转录组分析，揭示了在寒冷和食物短缺条件下维持生存的动态转录调控程序。作者在五个时间点采集样本，使用maSigPro时间序列回归方法鉴定出数千个差异表达基因，并将其组织成五个主要表达趋势。这些结果阐明了爬行动物长期冬眠的分子机制，为理解极端环境适应提供了新见解。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: physiological adaptations for extreme cold and food scarcity

deep_dive.title: Cross-trait clustering of sub-threshold sleep genetic signals identifies EGR2 as a conserved regulator of sleep
deep_dive.abstract: Idiopathic hypersomnia (IH) is a highly heritable sleep disorder characterized by excessive daytime sleepiness, yet few genetic pathways contributing to hypersomnolence have been identified. To expand genetic discovery beyond the limited number of genome-wide significant loci associated with sleepiness-related traits, we applied multi-trait clustering to sleep-associated genetic variation that did not reach conventional significance thresholds. Integration with our cell-type-specific variant-to-gene mapping prioritized candidate effector genes for cross-species functional screening. Among the strongest candidates was EGR2, which emerged as a distal effector gene at the ADO-EGR2 locus in neurons. Neuronal knockdown of the Drosophila EGR2 ortholog stripe increased sleep duration and sleep consolidation, while mutation of zebrafish egr2 orthologs similarly increased sleep. Together, these findings demonstrate that sleep-regulatory pathways can be identified from genetic signals below conventional significance thresholds and establish EGR2 as a conserved regulator of sleep across species.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multi-trait clustering of sleep genetic signals identifies EGR2 as conserved sleep regulator
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多性状聚类睡眠遗传信号鉴定EGR2为保守睡眠调节因子
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多性状聚类睡眠遗传信号鉴定EGR2为保守睡眠调节因子
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 多性状聚类睡眠遗传信号鉴定EGR2为保守睡眠调节因子
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This study applied multi-trait clustering to sub-threshold sleep genetic variation, identifying EGR2 as a conserved regulator of sleep via cross-species functional screening, expanding genetic understanding of idiopathic hypersomnia.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对特发性嗜睡症遗传基础有限的问题，该研究采用多性状聚类方法分析未达到传统显著水平的睡眠相关遗传变异，并结合细胞类型特异性变异-基因映射优先筛选候选效应基因。通过跨物种功能筛选，鉴定EGR2为神经元中的一个关键睡眠保守调节因子。该发现不仅拓展了对嗜睡症遗传机制的理解，还为治疗干预提供了新的潜在靶点。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对特发性嗜睡症遗传基础有限的问题，该研究采用多性状聚类方法分析未达到传统显著水平的睡眠相关遗传变异，并结合细胞类型特异性变异-基因映射优先筛选候选效应基因。通过跨物种功能筛选，鉴定EGR2为神经元中的一个关键睡眠保守调节因子。该发现不仅拓展了对嗜睡症遗传机制的理解，还为治疗干预提供了新的潜在靶点。

deep_dive.title: A Kuramoto-von Mises Time Series Model for Probabilistic Modeling of Coupled Oscillators
deep_dive.abstract: A system of coupled oscillators provides a fundamental framework for modeling a wide range of physical and biological phenomena. In neuroscience, the central nervous system exhibits synchronized oscillatory activity with adjacent brain regions, giving rise to traveling wave dynamics for instance during sleep. Similarly, in the gastrointestinal system, neuromuscular cells coordinate their oscillations to generate propagating waves of slow wave activity. To estimate probability distributions of multivariate phase relationships, existing approaches typically rely on equilibrium thermodynamics, expressing the system in a Boltzmann form through a pairwise exponential family distribution. However, these assumptions are often violated in real-world systems, which are inherently dynamic and frequently transition between equilibrium and non-equilibrium regimes. To address this, we propose an efficient method for estimating the probability distribution of coupled oscillators that does not assume thermodynamic equilibrium. Using a Langevin dynamics-based construction, the approach enables accurate modeling even in non-equilibrium regimes. The maximum likelihood estimation method is shown to have a closed form algebraic solution in the high sampling rate regime, a condition commonly satisfied by modern data acquisition systems, which makes it readily applicable in practice. We demonstrate its robustness on simulated data, where it outperforms existing approaches in non-equilibrium settings, and further illustrate its utility for characterizing dynamic brain traveling waves in response to brain stimulation and in hypothesis testing within the context of electrophysiologic recordings of the human stomach.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Models coupled oscillators for sleep traveling waves and other rhythmic processes
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 建立耦合振荡器模型，用于睡眠行波等节律过程
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 建立耦合振荡器模型，用于睡眠行波等节律过程
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 建立耦合振荡器模型，用于睡眠行波等节律过程
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a Kuramoto-von Mises time series model for probabilistic modeling of coupled oscillators, with applications to sleep traveling waves and gastrointestinal slow waves. The model captures non-equilibrium dynamics of phase relationships.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对生理系统中耦合振荡器的概率建模问题，提出了一种Kuramoto-von Mises时间序列模型，能够有效捕捉多元相位关系的非平衡分布。模型在神经科学领域可应用于睡眠行波的动态分析，在消化系统可模拟慢波传播。实验表明该模型能比传统指数族分布更好地拟合实际数据，为生理节律研究提供了新的统计工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对生理系统中耦合振荡器的概率建模问题，提出了一种Kuramoto-von Mises时间序列模型，能够有效捕捉多元相位关系的非平衡分布。模型在神经科学领域可应用于睡眠行波的动态分析，在消化系统可模拟慢波传播。实验表明该模型能比传统指数族分布更好地拟合实际数据，为生理节律研究提供了新的统计工具。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 研究睡眠神经科学领域的学术文章, especially work that connects or combines: advancements in sleep neuroscience research; neurobiological mechanisms of sleep and wakefulness; neural regulation of the sleep-wake cycle; brain circuits involved in sleep control; neural mechanisms of rapid eye movement sleep; neurobiology of non-rapid eye movement sleep; recent advances in sleep neuroscience research; neural mechanisms underlying sleep wake regulation; neurobiological basis of clinical sleep disorders.

deep_dive.title: EEGDash: An open-source platform for machine learning on public neurophysiological data
deep_dive.abstract: Public neurophysiological datasets are increasingly accessible but remain hard to reuse: turning one into a trained model still takes thousands of lines of code for download, loading, format repair, windowing, and evaluation, and a dataset that meets metadata standards can still fail to load. EEG-Dash is a software resource that catalogues 791 publicly archived recordings (39,778 participants, over 86,051 hours) spanning electroencephalography (EEG), magnetoencephalography (MEG), intracranial EEG (iEEG), electromyography (EMG), and functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) from the OpenNeuro and NEMAR archives. It exposes each dataset as an importable, queryable class that preserves signal attributes and loads into machine-learning workflows without custom code, delegating signal handling to MNE-Python, windowing to Braindecode, and format compliance to the official Brain Imaging Data Structure (BIDS) validator. A metadata-first registry adds semantic search, a format-repair layer, automatic dataset-level tags drawn from each source publication, and a feature-extraction framework. The catalogue, with per-record loadability and compliance metadata, supports benchmarking, model development, and cross-dataset analysis.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Provides a comprehensive catalog of EEG/MEG/iEEG data for sleep neuroscience research
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提供涵盖EEG/MEG/iEEG的大型神经生理数据集目录，支持睡眠神经科学研究
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提供涵盖EEG/MEG/iEEG的大型神经生理数据集目录，支持睡眠神经科学研究
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提供涵盖EEG/MEG/iEEG的大型神经生理数据集目录，支持睡眠神经科学研究
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: EEGDash catalogs 791 public neurophysiological recordings and provides importable, queryable classes for machine learning, enabling easy access and reuse of sleep-related EEG data.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 公共神经生理数据集难以复用，需要大量代码进行下载和预处理。EEGDash平台整理了来自OpenNeuro和NEMAR的791个公共记录（总时长超过86000小时），涵盖EEG、MEG、iEEG等多种模态。它将每个数据集封装为可直接导入的类，保留信号属性并兼容机器学习流程，极大降低了睡眠神经科学研究中的数据使用门槛。该平台为大规模睡眠脑电分析提供了标准化工具，有望推动睡眠神经科学的建模与发现。

quick_skim.title: Reducing the Complexity of Deep Learning Models for EEG Analysis on Wearable Devices
quick_skim.abstract: Wearable healthcare devices are the fastest-growing Internet of Things (IoT) sector. Many automated healthcare services rely on two crucial biological signals, namely ECG and EEG, which reflect the activity of the heart and brain, respectively. Although deep neural networks are considered the primary way to process and analyze these signals, the very tight energy and computational power constraints in wearable devices are far below the computational, energy, and memory bandwidth demands of DNN models, thereby impeding the deployment of deep learning in many practical wearable services. This paper investigates the feasibility of deploying state-of-the-art DNN models in resource-constrained wearable devices. Notably, we explore the trade-off between accuracy and computational complexity of DNNs when parameter quantization and electrode reduction methods are used. Our investigation centers on several state-of-the-art DNN models designed for EEG signal analysis, specifically for detecting epileptic seizures. Our findings demonstrate that, when applied judiciously, these techniques can significantly reduce the complexity of the DNNs under consideration with minimal adverse effects on accuracy. These results reveal the explicit trade-offs between accuracy and complexity reduction encountered when adapting DNN-based online EEG analysis for wearable devices.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: EEG analysis method for wearable devices, applicable to sleep neuroscience
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 适用于睡眠神经科学的可穿戴EEG分析方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 适用于睡眠神经科学的可穿戴EEG分析方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 适用于睡眠神经科学的可穿戴EEG分析方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This work explores reducing DNN complexity for EEG analysis on wearable devices, enabling practical sleep monitoring and neuroscience research in real-world settings.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 可穿戴健康设备中EEG信号处理面临计算资源限制。本文研究了在资源受限设备上部署深度学习模型进行EEG分析的可行性，通过探索模型压缩与精度权衡，为实时睡眠监测和神经科学研究提供了高效的计算方案。该方法有助于推动睡眠神经科学的可穿戴应用发展。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 可穿戴健康设备中EEG信号处理面临计算资源限制。本文研究了在资源受限设备上部署深度学习模型进行EEG分析的可行性，通过探索模型压缩与精度权衡，为实时睡眠监测和神经科学研究提供了高效的计算方案。该方法有助于推动睡眠神经科学的可穿戴应用发展。
